methods.description: Protocol for sampling adult specimens of B. marginata from the Bay of Biscay and labelling them using the calcein-tagging method to distinguish newly calcified shells from original adults.

steps.description: adult specimens of B. marginata, sampled in the Bay of Biscay at 450 and 650 m depth, were used in different experiments
steps.searchable_summary: Sample adult B. marginata specimens from specific depths in the Bay of Biscay

steps.description: Before their introduction in the experiments, adults specimens were labelled using calcein-tagging method (Bernhard et al., 2004) in order to distinguish specimens that totally calcified their shells in our controlled experiments (not fluorescent) from the adults initially added (partly fluorescent).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply calcein-tagging to adult B. marginata specimens to distinguish new growth

methods.description: Setting up two different laboratory systems (closed system and Petri dish system) to culture B. marginata under stable physico-chemical conditions.

steps.description: a closed system (CS I and CS II ), with 25 l microfiltrated (0.45 µm) natural seawater circulating through a reservoir and different experiment bottles, and
steps.searchable_summary: Set up closed laboratory culture system with circulating microfiltrated seawater
outputs: Closed culture system (CS I, CS II)

steps.description: a Petri dish system (PD) where half of the seawater was renewed twice per week.
steps.searchable_summary: Set up Petri dish culture system with regular seawater renewal

methods.description: Maintaining B. marginata cultures in laboratory systems, including introducing adults, feeding, and allowing for reproduction and juvenile growth over a specified period.

steps.description: Between 30 and 190 adult specimens of B. marginata were introduced in each experiment, which were regularly fed with fresh Phaeodactylum tricornutum diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: Introduce adult B. marginata specimens into culture systems

steps.description: which were regularly fed with fresh Phaeodactylum tricornutum diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: Regularly feed foraminifera culture with diatoms

steps.description: In all experiments, which lasted from 43 to 108 days, we obtained production and growth of juveniles of Bulimina marginata.
steps.searchable_summary: Maintain culture experiment for specified duration to obtain reproduction and growth of juveniles

methods.description: Selecting specimens of Bulimina marginata that entirely calcified under controlled laboratory conditions for isotopic composition measurement, identified by their non-fluorescent state after calcein-tagging.

steps.description: Therefore, the isotopic composition of foraminiferal calcite was measured on tests of Bulimina marginata entirely calcified under controlled laboratory conditions (not fluorescent specimens).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify B. marginata specimens entirely calcified under controlled conditions based on non-fluorescence
Select non-fluorescent B. marginata specimens for isotopic analysis

methods.description: Monitoring key physico-chemical parameters (temperature, pH, alkalinity) within the laboratory culture systems.

steps.description: Temperature was recorded inside the incubators (standard deviations range from 0.1 to 1.1
decrease of pH by 0.3 units between the start and the end (average of 7.79±0.09,NBS-scale) occurred in the six experiments,
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor pH levels in culture systems

steps.description: whereas alkalinity remained stable, and similar to the other systems (2487±14 µmol l −1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: Monitor alkalinity levels in culture systems
